keyEvents.event: CIA-backed coup against Prime Minister Mossadegh
keyEvents.impact: Seeded enduring distrust of the United States and fear of foreign-engineered regime change
Established clerical rule under velayat-e faqih and a foreign policy of revolutionary export

keyEvents.event: End of the eight-year Iran-Iraq War
keyEvents.impact: A million casualties taught reliance on self-sufficiency, missiles, and proxies over conventional parity

keyEvents.event: UN sanctions over the nuclear program
keyEvents.impact: Decades of escalating sanctions hardened a resistance economy and siege mentality
Brief sanctions relief in exchange for enrichment limits; showed diplomacy could pay

keyEvents.event: US withdrawal from the JCPOA and maximum pressure
keyEvents.impact: Vindicated hardliners, crashed the economy, and pushed enrichment toward weapons-grade

keyEvents.event: US assassination of IRGC commander Qasem Soleimani
keyEvents.impact: Demonstrated vulnerability of the proxy architecture and deepened confrontation with Washington

history.narrative: A revolutionary theocracy that compensates for conventional weakness with ballistic missiles, nuclear hedging, and an axis of armed proxies stretching from Lebanon to Yemen. Sanctions and isolation have bred a resilient siege economy and a leadership convinced that only deterrence and forward defense keep the regime alive.

redLines.description: Foreign-backed regime change attempts or support for internal uprisings
Military attack on Iranian nuclear facilities
Direct strikes on Iranian soil or assassination of senior leadership

internalDivisions.name: Reformist-hardliner struggle over the revolution's direction
Kurdish, Baloch, Azeri, and Arab minority grievances
Secular youth alienation from clerical rule

goals.description: Achieve a nuclear deterrent to guarantee regime survival
Counter Israeli power and intelligence operations through the axis of resistance
Export the Islamic Revolution and sustain proxy networks in Lebanon, Iraq, Syria, and Yemen
Break sanctions isolation through energy ties with China and Russia
